data.narrative:
Reliable Robotics is a Series C-stage company in Space & Aerospace that has raised $134M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of five private peers at the same stage—Axiom Space, Impulse Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, and Isar Aerospace—valued between $1.0B and $2.5B, plus five public aerospace benchmarks (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) trading at a median P/S of 53.2x.

Two valuation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $134M total raised as a proxy valuation and applying a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, implies $228M. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a $1.5B median valuation, with a range of $1.0B to $2.5B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures, which private companies typically do not publish.

The triangulated range is $194M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $864M across the two applicable methods. This wide dispersion reflects tension between the stage-progression method—which uses only capital raised history—and peer multiples, which embed market sentiment about aerospace valuations at Series C maturity. Key uncertainties include whether Reliable Robotics commands a premium or discount relative to the peer cohort, and the degree to which public-market multiples (53.2x P/S) are achievable at private-company scale.
